brand.name: MEC
brand.positioning: Operations command center by Jarvis AI.

supplyIntel.headline: Supply-market intelligence
supplyIntel.subline: Market-driven price forecast and risk alerts per supplier and sourcing country — built from news, FX and your real purchase history, refreshed every 12 hours with every claim backed by a dated source.
supplyIntel.noCrisesHint: No risk signals in the latest scan across your suppliers and countries.
supplyIntel.actWhy_secure: High risk or a sharp ~{pct}% rise is likely — lock in {commodity} volume now and line up a backup supplier before price or availability moves.
supplyIntel.actWhy_order: Prices are trending up ~{pct}% — place {commodity} orders soon to lock today's cost before the increase lands.
supplyIntel.actWhy_wait: Prices are easing ~{pct}% — hold non-urgent {commodity} buying; you may secure a better cost shortly.
supplyIntel.actWhy_monitor: {commodity} is stable with no strong signal — keep normal ordering and watch for changes.
supplyIntel.noRisks: No active risk signals in the latest scan.
supplyIntel.emptyHint: The Supply-Market Intelligence workflow runs every 12 hours. Create the Supabase tables (supabase/schema.sql) so results can be stored — the next run will populate this page.
supplyIntel.source: Source: n8n Supply-Market Intelligence → Supabase supply_intel
supplyIntel.note: Each forecast and risk is synthesised by JARVIS from recent news and official advisories; any claim without a real, dated source is dropped before it reaches this page. Recommendations are advisory — a human decides every purchase.

whatsapp.headline: WhatsApp inbox
whatsapp.subline: Incoming WhatsApp messages from clients via WaSender — classified by intent and parsed for orders, in one place.
whatsapp.emptyHint: Point your WaSender webhook at the MEC WhatsApp Intake workflow and create the Supabase tables (supabase/schema.sql). Incoming messages will appear here, classified by intent.
whatsapp.source: Source: WaSender → n8n WhatsApp Intake → Supabase whatsapp_intake
whatsapp.note: Messages are received via WaSender, classified by JARVIS (order / inquiry / complaint) and parsed for products. Nothing is auto-approved — every order is reviewed by a person.

analytics.headline: Analytics
analytics.subline: Live dashboards from MEC's real sales and procurement data — Oct 2025 to Jun 2026.
analytics.reconcileNote: Revenue = Collected + Receivables (all figures include 15% VAT). The same totals appear on the Control Center and the Clients page — every number is computed once from the sales workbooks.
analytics.procNote: Note: supplier payables and warehouse dwell-time were not filled in the source sheets, so those views are omitted.
analytics.sellVsCost: Sell price vs cost by category
analytics.marginNote: Margin = average sell unit price (sales) vs average buy unit cost (purchases) per category.
analytics.profitDiagramSub: Each product's sell price split into cost vs profit (per carton). The marker is the minimum-margin floor.
analytics.profitNote: Gross profit per product = (sell price per carton − matched purchase cost per carton) × cartons sold, on a pre-VAT basis. Cost is matched to each product's own purchase line by name. Minimum margins: meat 3%, chicken 5%, vegetables 6%, potatoes 10%. Products with no matching purchase line show '—' (cost not available) and are excluded from totals.

approvals.headline: Order approvals
approvals.subline: Orders captured from WhatsApp via WaSender, parsed by JARVIS — approve or reject each before it goes to the warehouse.
approvals.noProducts: No products parsed — review the message.
approvals.confirmDelete: Delete this order permanently? It will be removed from approvals, documents, the orders feed and notifications.
approvals.emptyHint: New WhatsApp orders appear here automatically, parsed into line items, ready to approve.
approvals.source: Source: WhatsApp (WaSender) → JARVIS classification → Supabase whatsapp_intake
approvals.note: Orders are captured and parsed automatically; nothing is auto-approved — a human approves or rejects every order. Run supabase/schema.sql once to enable approve/reject persistence.

automations.headline: Automations
automations.sublineAdmin: The n8n workflows that run MEC's operation. Tune each workflow's cadence or pause it — there is no manual run; workflows fire on their own schedule or event.
automations.internalSub: Run on MEC's own data & operations.
automations.externalSub: Pull data in from the outside world.
automations.noManualBody: Workflows are not run by hand. Scheduled ones fire on the interval you set; event ones fire when their trigger happens. This keeps runs auditable and prevents accidental double-processing.
automations.activitySub: Every message this automation read — for audit and debugging. Raw payloads included.
automations.logEmptyHint: Messages this automation processes will appear here as they arrive.
automations.noLogBody: This automation ships in its phase. Once live, every item it processes is logged here for audit.

jarvis.subtitle: Ask anything about MEC
jarvis.greeting: Hi — I'm JARVIS. Ask me about MEC's sales, margins, receivables, suppliers or products.
jarvis.reportNoAccess: I couldn't build that report — it needs data your role can't access.

documents.headline: Order documents
documents.subline: Every order needs its PO, invoice, delivery note and payment proof. JARVIS reads them from WhatsApp and flags whatever's missing.
documents.note: The order message is the PO. Invoice, delivery note and payment proof are matched from later document messages by the same sender. Missing ones are flagged and pushed to notifications — once JARVIS is in the MEC WhatsApp group it reads the group's documents automatically.
documents.noFile: No file attached — this was a text message.
documents.fileNote: The file is decrypted from WhatsApp on demand. Invoices, delivery notes and payment proofs are only counted when sent as an actual signed/stamped file.
documents.emptyHint: Orders arrive via WhatsApp; their documents are tracked here.

report.title: Report
report.noReport: No report to show. Generate one from the dashboard or ask JARVIS.

clients.headline: Clients
clients.subline: {count} clients that transacted with MEC — {erp} from your ERP file + {sales} seen only in sales. Search, risk and revenue at a glance.
clients.searchPh: Search by name, ID or city…
clients.directorySub: Click any client to open their full file — products, orders and credit.

products.headline: Products
products.subline: Every product MEC sells — demand, price, cost and margin. Click any product for its full page.
products.catalogSub: Click any product to open its page.
products.onHandUnrec: Unreconciled — ledger doesn't balance for this SKU

productDetail.priceTrendSub: Avg sell price vs matched purchase cost per month.
productDetail.noData: No data for this product yet.
productDetail.alertsNote: Live supply-market intelligence for this product's commodity, refreshed daily by the intelligence workflow. Each alert is sourced.
productDetail.noAlerts: No market alerts for this product's commodity today.
productDetail.whUnrecNote: The warehouse ledger under-records outbound for this SKU, so this on-hand isn't reliable until the missing issues are logged — see Inventory → Unreconciled.
productDetail.whNone: This product isn't matched to a warehouse SKU in the current ledger, so live on-hand isn't available.

clientDetail.creditIndicative: Credit limit is derived from the client’s peak monthly purchasing until MEC’s finance system provides real limits. Used = actual uncollected balance.
clientDetail.monthlyTrendSub: Monthly sales to this client (incl. VAT).
clientDetail.noData: No data for this client yet.

orders.headline: Orders
orders.subline: Live order book across all clients — status, margin and payment due in one view.
orders.byStatusSub: Where the order book stands now.

latest.title: Latest orders & proofs
latest.subtitle: Newest orders from WhatsApp, by date & time — with their proof documents. Live: deleting an order removes it everywhere.
latest.emptyHint: Orders sent to the WhatsApp group appear here automatically, newest first.
latest.confirmDelete: Delete this order permanently? It will be removed from every page — approvals, documents, the orders feed and notifications.

common.noAccessBody: Your role doesn't include this area. Contact your administrator if you need access.

profile.forgot: Forgot password — generate a new one
profile.tooShort: New password must be at least 6 characters.
profile.storeUnavailable: User store not connected — run the Supabase schema first.

ops.headline: Operations house
ops.subline: The whole operation at a glance for {date} — orders, approvals, delays, payments and the warehouse.
ops.warehouseSub: Stock on hand against the 6,000-carton capacity.
ops.warehouseNote: On-hand is netted from the warehouse ledger and is indicative — unrecorded issues inflate it (see the data concern). Open Inventory for per-SKU stock, expiry and reorder points.

credit.headline: Credit (المديونية)
credit.subline: What clients currently owe MEC — per invoice and per client, with aging. The authoritative receivables statement.
credit.sourceBody: These balances come from the المديونية statement Tarek sends the team on WhatsApp, dated {date}. The portal's receivables (Control Center, CRM, Collections) reconcile to this total. Amounts are VAT-inclusive — tap the VAT chip to see ex-VAT.
credit.agingSub: Outstanding by how long it has been due.
credit.byClientSub: Outstanding grouped by client (click a name for the full file).
credit.byInvoiceSub: Every open invoice on the statement.
credit.jarvisIntro: Potential issues in this statement and how to act on them.
credit.n_overdue_b: These are the highest collection risk — assign an owner, contact the client this week, and consider holding new credit until paid.
credit.n_conc_b: A large share of outstanding sits with one client. Set a credit limit for them and diversify exposure to reduce the impact of a single late payment.
credit.n_tip_t: Chase 0–7 day balances before they age
credit.n_tip_b: Most balances here are still current. A short reminder before day 7 keeps the average age low and protects cash flow.

inventory.headline: Inventory
inventory.subline: Per-SKU stock on hand, expiry and reorder points — netted from the warehouse ledger against the 6,000-carton capacity.
inventory.recordedLine: Ledger records net +{net} cartons in−out (raw {raw}); {excluded} SKU(s) excluded as unreconciled.
inventory.note: On-hand = received − issued from the warehouse ledger. {excluded} SKU(s) record far more received than issued (outbound not logged) — counting their net would push stock past the 6,000 capacity, so they're excluded from the total and flagged below. Fix = log the missing issues for those SKUs. Expiry is estimated from the last receipt + typical frozen shelf life until real batch dates are wired.
inventory.footNote: {excl} flagged SKU(s) excluded — ledger doesn't balance
inventory.countSub: Tarek's stock count, {date} · {n} items — compared against the ledger on-hand.
inventory.countNote: These are the physical quantities Tarek reported on WhatsApp. The ledger on-hand is netted from recorded in/out movements; a large variance flags either unlogged movements or a recount — investigate those rows. Matching to ledger SKUs is by name similarity (≈ shows the matched SKU).
inventory.jarvisIntro: Potential data issues in the warehouse and how to fix them.
inventory.n_var_t: {n} item(s) differ sharply from the ledger (worst: {item}, {v})
inventory.n_var_b: A big gap usually means outbound movements weren't logged, or the count needs redoing. Reconcile these SKUs first — the on-hand figures driving reorder decisions depend on them.
inventory.n_unrec_b: These record far more received than issued, so their on-hand is excluded from the total. Log the missing outbound movements to restore an accurate balance.
inventory.n_nomatch_t: {n} counted item(s) have no ledger match
inventory.n_nomatch_b: Add these products to the SKU master (or align the names) so the physical count reconciles automatically next time.
inventory.n_reorder_t: {n} SKU(s) at or below reorder point
inventory.n_reorder_b: Plan purchase orders for these ahead of lead time to avoid st-outs on fast movers.

admin.subline: Manage test data before launch. Clearing wipes the rows in Supabase — use it while testing, not in production.
admin.testingBody: WhatsApp messages, orders and inquiries here are test data. Clear them so the launch starts clean. WhatsApp test data is archived (hidden from the portal but kept in the automation log for audit); contact and internal messages are deleted.
admin.notConfigured: Supabase isn't connected, so there's nothing to clear.
admin.d_whatsapp: Inbound messages + order-approval queue. Archived, not deleted — kept in the automation log.
admin.footnote: Only the CEO and admin can see this console. Deletions are permanent and cannot be undone.

adminData.title: Data
adminData.subline: The latest data fetched from every source — WhatsApp groups, email and supply intelligence — with which channel it came from, the sender, the message, and whether JARVIS captured it into the portal.
adminData.missedBody: {n} message(s) mention quantities or prices but weren't captured as orders. Open them below (highlighted) and, if they're real orders, place them in the approvals queue. Tighten the classifier so they're caught automatically.
adminData.feedSub: Newest first, across all sources. Tap a message to expand it.
adminData.emptyHint: Items fetched by the n8n workflows (WhatsApp, email, supply intelligence) appear here.
adminData.note: "Captured" means JARVIS turned the item into a structured portal record — an order, an approval, a document, or a supply forecast. "Not captured" items were received but classified as general chatter; they still appear in the WhatsApp inbox but aren't actioned. Use this page to spot relevant data the automation missed.

messages.headline: JARVIS inbox
messages.subline: Message your MEC colleagues directly inside the portal.
messages.notConfiguredHint: Run the Supabase schema (internal_messages table) to enable staff messaging.

login.headline: Welcome back
login.subline: Sign in to the MEC operations portal.
login.credsHelper: Use the username and password assigned to you by your administrator.
login.errorRequired: Please enter your username and password.

control.headline: MEC operations overview
control.subline: Real figures from MEC's sales, clients and orders. The operational automation modules are planned below.
control.opsLayerBody: These modules are the blueprint for MEC's automated operations. Each opens to its requirement and planned workflow — they activate once connected to live sources via the Automations hub.
control.automationEmptyHint: Live decisions and exceptions will appear here once the n8n workflows are connected.
control.executionLogSub: What the system has been doing.

dept.requirementBody: Each module below is part of MEC's planned operations. Open one to see its requirement, the metrics it will track, and the planned workflow.

solution.noActivityHint: Decisions, exceptions and the activity log appear here once this module is connected to its sources.

notifDetail.notFound: This message is no longer available.

savings.blueprintSub: What MEC will save in cost and hours by running operations through the platform instead of manual WhatsApp, email and spreadsheets.
savings.requirementBody: Once the automation modules run, this page measures the real cost and time saved vs the old manual process — by department and overall. No figures are shown until there is data.
savings.emptyHint: Savings are calculated automatically once the n8n automations are connected and running.

academy.headline: Learn the MEC platform
academy.subline: Short guides for every team — sales, approvals, warehouse, logistics and finance.
academy.gettingStartedBody: Log in, read your dashboard, and follow an order from capture to payment.
academy.approvalsBody: How the approval assistant scores margin, stock and payment risk before you decide.
academy.documentsBody: How email and WhatsApp documents are captured, classified and matched to orders.
academy.maintenanceBody: The Academy is in beta and still under construction. Guides and videos are being prepared — content here is placeholder and will be filled in soon.

contact.headline: Talk to Jarvis AI
contact.subline: Questions, changes or a new workflow for MEC — reach the team.
contact.name: Your name
contact.sentNote: Thanks — your message was sent to the Jarvis team on WhatsApp and email.
contact.errNote: Couldn't send right now. Please try again or email partners@jarvisksa.com.
contact.routedNote: Submissions are sent instantly to the Jarvis AI team via WhatsApp and email.
